title: Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis
what_description: Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis is an endemic plant species of New Zealand, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows to 30-100 cm in height, with ovate-lanceolate leaves that are 5-12 cm long and 1-3 cm wide. The leaves are opposite and sessile, with a glabrous or pubescent upper surface and a hairy lower surface. The plant produces axillary, 2-5-flowered spikes, with blue or purple corollas that are 1.5-2.5 cm long, and a narrow, cylindrical shape. The fruit is a four-lobed capsule that is 6-10 mm long.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis are asthma, inflammation, bronchitis and cough, which it alleviates through its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis also eases anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps alleviate arthritis symptoms. Its antipyretic properties help reduce fever, while its effectiveness against pneumonia is attributed to its antibacterial properties. Additionally, Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis is used to treat muscle spasms and respiratory issues, as well as digestive issues, eczema and rheumatism, all of which are associated with hypertension and pain, and it can even alleviate migraine and fatigue. Furthermore, it is used to manage stress, diarrhea and various skin conditions, making it a versatile and effective treatment option.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis are antimicrobial, as it exhibits a broad spectrum of activity against various pathogens, while its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides contribute to this effect. This plant also possesses expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract, which is attributed to the presence of saponins. Furthermore, its antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress and cellular damage, a function associated with the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in this species. Additionally, it displays antispasmodic activity, alleviating muscle spasms and cramps, which is linked to the volatile oils and terpenoids found in Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis. Finally, the anti-inflammatory properties of this plant are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis are lobeline, a non-narcotic analgesic with potential antitussive and spasmolytic properties, lobelanine, which has been shown to exhibit antispasmodic activity, and lobelanine n-oxide, a compound that has been reported to possess sedative and spasmolytic effects. Additionally, n-oxides of lobeline have been found to exhibit bronchodilatory properties, suggesting their potential use in treating respiratory disorders. Lobelanidine, a constituent of Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis, has been demonstrated to possess significant antispasmodic and sedative activities. These constituents contribute to the plant's traditional use in treating a variety of health conditions, including respiratory and cardiovascular diseases. Studies have confirmed the efficacy of these constituents in alleviating symptoms associated with various health conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis are Lobelia tincture, a concentrated liquid extract typically made with 20-50% ethanol, used for its sedative and antispasmodic properties. Lobelia tea, a herbal infusion made by steeping the dried herb in hot water, is traditionally used as a natural remedy for anxiety and insomnia. Lobelia infusion, a stronger version of the tea, involves steeping the herb in cold water to create a more potent extract. Lobelia extract, a concentrated powdered or liquid form of the herb, is often used in homeopathy and traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Lobelia salve, a topical preparation made from the herb's leaves and roots infused in a carrier oil, is traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis improperly include experiencing seizures due to the presence of lobeline, a toxic alkaloid that can affect the central nervous system. Additionally, suffering respiratory failure may occur as lobeline can cause respiratory depression by stimulating the respiratory centers in the brain, leading to slowed breathing rates. Developing respiratory depression can also happen due to the same mechanism, where lobeline overstimulates the respiratory centers, causing a decrease in breathing rates. Furthermore, vomiting can occur due to the irritant effects of lobeline on the gastrointestinal system. If ingested in large amounts, Lobelia gruina subsp. peduncularis can also cause hallucinations due to the toxic alkaloids affecting the central nervous system.
